scope.summary:
Goal: implement the editors <-> Locus (Pillar 6, structured work tracking) interop edge. Locus is the project's structured work-tracking pillar: it holds Work Packet (WP) and Microtask (MT) records (e.g. WP-KERNEL-012, MT-034). This MT lets code comments and rich notes cross-reference those governed work units through a `locus://` addressing scheme that sits ALONGSIDE the existing addressing already built in this WP: the MT-032 loom/everything-is-a-block scheme (`loom://` / block addressing) and the MT-034 code<->note cross-ref node pattern (`code:` chips + the CrossRef node + resolution service). Concretely: (A) a note or code comment can write `locus://wp/WP-KERNEL-012` or `locus://mt/MT-034`, the editor renders it as a clickable Locus chip, and clicking navigates to that WP/MT record through the shell navigation bus (the MT-030 navigation bus reused via the WP-011 command bus); (B) a reverse-lookup surface answers 'which documents/code reference this WP/MT?' so a Locus panel can list referencing documents for a given work unit. This edge is READ + REFERENCE ONLY: the editors never create, mutate, transition, or delete Locus WP/MT records; they only resolve a `locus://` ref to a record title/summary and navigate to it, and enumerate inbound references.

This MT is a parallel sibling of MT-034 (code<->note cross-refs). It REUSES, does not re-invent: (1) the MT-032 addressing/normalization scheme (the everything-is-a-block URI form and its parse/normalize helpers) — extend it with a `locus://` scheme variant rather than building a new parser; (2) the MT-034 cross-reference node + resolution-service pattern (the CrossRef inline node rendered as a chip, plus the MT-034 cross-ref resolution service in `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/cross_refs.rs` and the `dispatch_command` open-* seam routed through the WP-011 `command_registry.rs` + `event_bus.rs` command bus) — add a Locus variant to that same machinery instead of a parallel one; (3) the MT-030 navigation bus / WP-011 command bus (`command_registry.rs` + `event_bus.rs`) for navigation; (4) the WP-011 shell primitives: the pane registry (`pane_registry.rs`), the command bus (`command_registry.rs` + `event_bus.rs`), and the AccessKit id registry (`accessibility/registry.rs`). Do not duplicate any of those substrates.

Files to CREATE:
  - `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/interop/locus_interop.rs` (~250-400 lines) — the Locus interop module. Defines: `LocusRefKind = WorkPacket | Microtask`; `LocusRef { kind: LocusRefKind, raw: String, id: String, normalized: String }` (e.g. id="WP-KERNEL-012" or "MT-034"); `LocusRecord { kind: LocusRefKind, id: String, title: String, summary: Option<String>, status: Option<String> }` (the resolved, display-facing projection of a WP/MT). Provides a PURE parser `pub fn parse_locus_ref(raw: &str) -> Option<LocusRef>` that recognizes `locus://wp/{id}` and `locus://mt/{id}` (and the bare-id shorthand the MT-032 scheme already normalizes, kept consistent with that scheme). Provides the resolution service: `pub async fn resolve_locus_ref(client: &..., workspace_id: &str, r: &LocusRef) -> Result<LocusRecord, LocusInteropError>` which calls the bound READ APIs (WP -> GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/locus/work-packets/{id}, MT -> GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/locus/microtasks/{id}). Provides the reverse lookup `pub async fn find_documents_referencing(client: &..., workspace_id: &str, r: &LocusRef) -> Result<Vec<DocumentRef>, LocusInteropError>` that returns the documents/code blocks whose content carries a `locus://` ref to the given WP/MT (reuse the same backlink/search mechanism MT-034's `find_notes_referencing_symbol` uses, keyed on the normalized `locus://` ref value rather than re-implementing a scan). `LocusInteropError` MUST carry a distinct, typed `LocusReadApiUnavailable` variant for the typed-blocker path (see below). `DocumentRef { document_id: String, document_title: String, block_id: Option<String>, excerpt: String }` (match the MT-034 NoteRef shape so the two cross-ref surfaces stay symmetric).

Files to EDIT:
  - `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/interop/mod.rs` — add `pub mod locus_interop;` and export the public types (create the `interop` module dir + `mod.rs` if it does not yet exist; if the WP shell already roots interop modules elsewhere, place `locus_interop.rs` under that existing root and adjust the path accordingly rather than forking a new tree — record the chosen path in a code comment).
  - `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/cross_refs.rs` — extend the MT-034 cross-ref node machinery with a Locus variant: add a `locus_ref` inline node (attrs { kind: "wp"|"mt", id, normalized }) handled by the SAME CrossRef rendering/resolution path as the MT-034 code_ref node, rendered as a Locus chip (a rounded `egui::Frame` chip showing the short id, e.g. `WP-KERNEL-012` or `MT-034`, plus a small work-unit glyph). On click the chip fires `bus.dispatch_command("open-locus-ref", CommandArgs::LocusRef{ kind, id })` through the reused command bus; register `open-locus-ref` on the bus so the shell routes it to the Locus pane / record view via the MT-030 navigation bus (NavTarget for the WP/MT record). If the resolution returns the typed `LocusReadApiUnavailable`, render the chip in an 'unresolved' (greyed) state with a tooltip naming the missing endpoint, and do NOT panic. Reuse the MT-034 chip rendering and AccessKit registration helpers; add only the Locus-specific attrs and command id.

State / data model:
  - The `locus_ref` rich-text node persists attrs { kind, id, normalized } and MUST survive the rich-document save+reload round-trip exactly like the MT-034 code_ref node (declare `locus_ref` as an allowed node type; if the backend node-type validation rejects it, raise a typed blocker — do not silently strip).
  - Resolution results (`LocusRecord`) and reverse-lookup results (`Vec<DocumentRef>`) are cached on the owning panel/struct; resolution and reverse lookup run OFF the egui render path (async / via the existing client request mechanism) — never block the frame thread.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
  1. Parsing: `parse_locus_ref` is a PURE, side-effect-free function. Recognize `locus://wp/{id}` -> LocusRef{ kind: WorkPacket, id }, `locus://mt/{id}` -> LocusRef{ kind: Microtask, id }. Normalize the id form consistently with the MT-032 scheme's normalization (trim, case, separator rules); store the canonical form in `normalized` and use it as the single key for both resolution and reverse lookup so the two directions never disagree. Reuse MT-032 normalization helpers; do NOT add a second normalizer.
  2. Resolution: `resolve_locus_ref` dispatches on kind -> the matching bound READ endpoint. WorkPacket -> GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/locus/work-packets/{id}; Microtask -> GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/locus/microtasks/{id}. Map the response into `LocusRecord { title, summary, status }`. A 404 from a live endpoint means 'record not found' -> return an `Unresolved`/NotFound result (greyed chip), NOT the typed-blocker variant. The typed-blocker variant is reserved for the endpoint not existing on the frontend-reachable API surface at all (see step 7).
  3. Chip rendering: reuse the MT-034 CrossRef chip path. The Locus chip shows the short id text and a work-unit glyph; entire chip is a Button; click fires `open-locus-ref`. A resolved chip may show the resolved title on hover (tooltip) when `LocusRecord.title` is available. An unresolved/NotFound chip renders greyed with an 'unresolved' label. The 'API unavailable' chip renders greyed with a tooltip naming the missing endpoint.
  4. Navigation: register `open-locus-ref` on the InteractionBus (MT-031) and route it through the MT-030 navigation bus to the Locus record view / pane. Do NOT create a new navigation channel; pass navigation out via the same `FnMut(NavTarget)` / command-dispatch seam the WP shell already uses. The NavTarget identifies the WP/MT record so the shell can focus or open the Locus pane on that record.
  5. Reverse lookup (Locus panel inbound references): `find_documents_referencing` returns the documents/code blocks whose stored content carries a `locus://` ref to the given WP/MT. Reuse the MT-034 `find_notes_referencing_symbol` mechanism (backlink filter on ref_value, or knowledge search) keyed on the normalized `locus://` ref value. Provide this as a callable service so a Locus panel can render 'Referenced by N documents' for a selected WP/MT; the panel itself (full Locus pane UI) is downstream (MT-073) — THIS MT delivers the parser, resolution, reverse-lookup service, the `locus_ref` node + chip, and the navigation seam. De-duplicate results on (document_id, block_id).
  6. AccessKit: register every Locus chip and reverse-lookup row through the WP-011 AccessKit id registry (never ad-hoc). Chip author_id = `locus-ref-chip-{kind}-{id}` (e.g. `locus-ref-chip-wp-WP-KERNEL-012`), role = Button, action Press. Reverse-lookup rows (when surfaced) get author_id = `locus-refby-{document_id}`, role = ListItem. Ids must be stable across frames so swarm agents can address them deterministically; de-duplicate so no two nodes share an author_id.
  7. TYPED BLOCKER (hard requirement): if the Locus READ API is NOT exposed to the frontend-reachable surface (the work-packets / microtasks GET endpoints are absent from the bound handshake_core API surface, not merely returning 404 for a missing id), STOP and raise the typed `LocusInteropError::LocusReadApiUnavailable` blocker naming the exact missing endpoint(s). Do NOT add, scaffold, stub, or rewrite any backend endpoint; do NOT introduce a local SQLite cache or any non-Postgres store; do NOT mutate Locus state. Backend access is READ ONLY via existing handshake_core APIs; gaps are typed blockers. PostgreSQL/EventLedger only — no SQLite anywhere.
  8. Reuse, do not fork: extend the MT-032 addressing scheme and the MT-034 cross-ref node/resolution machinery; reuse the MT-030 navigation bus and WP-011 shell primitives (pane registry, command bus, AccessKit id registry). A grep gate should show the Locus path importing the MT-032 normalizer and the MT-034 CrossRef node/chip helpers rather than defining duplicates.

Backend API bound (REUSE ONLY — no new endpoints, no backend rewrite): GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/locus/work-packets/{id} (read a WP record; typed blocker `LocusReadApiUnavailable` if absent from the frontend-reachable surface) and GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/locus/microtasks/{id} (read an MT record). Reverse lookup reuses the MT-034 backlink/knowledge-search mechanism keyed on the normalized `locus://` ref value. If any required read capability is genuinely absent, raise the typed blocker — do not add or rewrite backend endpoints.

Dependencies / unblock conditions:
  - MT-030 (navigation bus) MUST exist — this MT routes `open-locus-ref` through it; no new navigation channel.
  - MT-032 (loom block addressing / everything-is-a-block scheme + normalization) MUST be merged — the `locus://` scheme extends it and reuses its normalizer.
  - MT-034 (code<->note cross-refs: CrossRef node + chip + resolution service + reverse-lookup mechanism) MUST be merged — this MT adds a Locus variant to that same machinery.
  - Backend: GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/locus/work-packets/{id} and GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/locus/microtasks/{id} must be reachable on the frontend API surface; if not, the typed blocker is the correct outcome.

Risk if missed: Notes and code cannot link to governed work units; the structured-work force-multiplier edge is missing. Code/notes stay disconnected from Locus WP/MT records, so a reader cannot jump from a comment to the work unit that governs it, and Locus cannot show which documents reference a given WP/MT. The 'melt-together' / cross-pillar interconnection claim is unproven for the editors<->Locus pair, and MT-073 (which builds the Locus panel UI on top of this service) is blocked.
acceptance_criteria: AC-001: `parse_locus_ref` is a PURE function recognizing `locus://wp/{id}` and `locus://mt/{id}` and producing a normalized id consistent with the MT-032 scheme — proven by `cargo test` over inputs `locus://wp/WP-KERNEL-012` and `locus://mt/MT-034` asserting kind, id, and normalized fields; an invalid scheme returns None.

AC-002: A `locus://` ref resolves to a WP/MT title via the bound READ API — proven by a test that calls `resolve_locus_ref` against GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/locus/work-packets/{id} (and the microtasks endpoint) and asserts `LocusRecord.title` is non-empty for a known record.

AC-003: A `locus_ref` node renders as a clickable Locus chip and navigates to the record via the reused MT-030 navigation bus — proven by kittest: click the chip and assert `open-locus-ref` is dispatched with the matching { kind, id } and routed through the navigation seam (no new navigation channel created).

AC-004: Reverse lookup lists documents referencing a given WP/MT — proven by a test: seed a document/code block containing `locus://mt/MT-034`, call `find_documents_referencing` for MT-034, assert the document appears (keyed on the normalized ref value; results de-duplicated on (document_id, block_id)).

AC-005: A missing Locus READ endpoint raises the typed blocker — proven by a test that simulates the work-packets/microtasks GET endpoint being absent from the reachable surface and asserts `LocusInteropError::LocusReadApiUnavailable` is returned naming the missing endpoint, with the chip rendering greyed (no panic).

AC-006: The `locus_ref` rich-text node survives save+reload with attrs { kind, id, normalized } intact (declared as an allowed node type) — proven by a round-trip test; a record-not-found (live-endpoint 404) renders a greyed 'unresolved' chip without panic.

AC-007: The Locus path reuses the MT-032 normalizer and the MT-034 CrossRef node/chip + resolution/reverse-lookup machinery; no duplicate parser, normalizer, navigation channel, or cross-ref node is defined — proven by grep showing the imports and the absence of duplicates.

AC-008: AccessKit nodes `locus-ref-chip-{kind}-{id}` (Button) and, where surfaced, `locus-refby-{document_id}` (ListItem) are present with correct roles and registered through the WP-011 AccessKit id registry; no duplicate author_id — proven by kittest AccessKit tree dump.

AC-009: Backend access is READ ONLY via the two bound GET endpoints (plus the reused MT-034 reverse-lookup mechanism); no `src/backend/**` files are modified, no new endpoints added, and no SQLite is introduced — proven by diff/dependency gate.
AC-010: `cargo test -p handshake-native test_locus_interop -- --nocapture` passes with no failures and no panics.
proof_targets: PT-001: `cargo test -p handshake-native test_locus_interop parse_locus_ref -- --nocapture` => `locus://wp/WP-KERNEL-012` and `locus://mt/MT-034` parse to the correct kind/id/normalized; invalid scheme => None; GREEN.

PT-002: integration/resolution test: `resolve_locus_ref` against GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/locus/work-packets/{id} and /locus/microtasks/{id} returns a `LocusRecord` with non-empty title for a known WP/MT; GREEN.
PT-003: kittest: render a `locus_ref` chip, click it, assert `open-locus-ref` dispatched with { kind, id } and routed through the MT-030 navigation bus (NavTarget for the record).
PT-004: reverse-lookup test: seed a document containing `locus://mt/MT-034`, call `find_documents_referencing(MT-034)`, assert the document is listed (de-duplicated); GREEN.

PT-005: typed-blocker test: simulate the Locus work-packets/microtasks GET endpoint absent from the reachable surface, assert `LocusInteropError::LocusReadApiUnavailable` naming the endpoint, chip renders greyed, no panic.
PT-006: round-trip test: rich doc with a `locus_ref` node saves and reloads with attrs { kind, id, normalized } preserved; GREEN.

PT-007: grep proof: MT-032 normalizer imported (no second normalizer); MT-034 CrossRef node/chip helpers reused (no duplicate cross-ref node); `open-locus-ref` routed via the existing command/navigation seam (no new nav channel); AccessKit ids registered via the WP-011 registry.
implementation_notes: Keep `parse_locus_ref` pure and side-effect-free (no I/O, no async) so it is trivially unit-testable; resolution and reverse lookup (which need backend reads) are separate steps that consume the pure parse output.
Use the normalized `locus://` ref value as the SINGLE shared key for resolution and reverse lookup so the two directions never disagree; reuse the MT-032 normalizer rather than adding a second one.

Mirror the MT-034 machinery exactly: the `locus_ref` node is a sibling of the MT-034 `code_ref` node and must go through the same CrossRef rendering/resolution/dispatch path; `DocumentRef` mirrors the MT-034 `NoteRef` shape so the two cross-ref surfaces stay symmetric.

Distinguish two failure modes precisely: live-endpoint 404 (record not found) -> greyed 'unresolved' chip, NOT a blocker; endpoint absent from the reachable API surface -> `LocusReadApiUnavailable` typed blocker. Tests must cover both.

Resolution and reverse lookup MUST run off the egui render path (async or via the existing client request mechanism used by MT-034); set a loading flag and store results on the struct; never block the frame thread.
Route `open-locus-ref` through the InteractionBus (MT-031) + MT-030 navigation bus via the existing `FnMut(NavTarget)` / dispatch seam; do not instantiate a new navigation channel or HTTP client.

Declare `locus_ref` as an explicit allowed rich-text node type so save+reload does not strip it; if backend node-type validation rejects it, raise a typed blocker rather than silently dropping the node.

Register every Locus chip and reverse-lookup-row author_id through the WP-011 AccessKit id registry; de-duplicate ids derived from (kind,id) and (document_id) so swarm agents address rows deterministically.

If the `interop` module root does not yet exist in the WP shell, create `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/interop/mod.rs`; if the shell already roots interop modules elsewhere, place the file under that root and document the chosen path in a code comment instead of forking a parallel tree.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/* Locus / work-packet reference surfaces — the React surfaces that display or link to WP/MT records: port the reference shape (how a WP/MT id is displayed, the chip/label form, and the click-to-open navigation behavior) into the native Locus chip + `open-locus-ref` seam; do not port any mutation/transition behavior (this MT is read+reference only).
binds_backend_api: GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/locus/work-packets/{id} — read a Work Packet record (title/summary/status) to resolve a `locus://wp/{id}` ref. REUSE ONLY (existing handshake_core API). Typed blocker `LocusInteropError::LocusReadApiUnavailable` if this endpoint is absent from the frontend-reachable surface.
GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/locus/microtasks/{id} — read a Microtask record to resolve a `locus://mt/{id}` ref. REUSE ONLY.
Reverse lookup ('which documents reference this WP/MT') reuses the MT-034 backlink/knowledge-search mechanism keyed on the normalized `locus://` ref value — no new endpoint.
No new endpoints, no backend rewrite, no Locus mutation. Backend access is READ ONLY; gaps are typed blockers. PostgreSQL/EventLedger only — no SQLite anywhere.

risks: RISK-001: A fresh `locus://` parser/normalizer is built inside locus_interop.rs instead of reusing the MT-032 scheme normalizer, causing key drift between resolution and reverse lookup and silent inconsistency between the two directions.

RISK-002: A parallel cross-ref node/chip/resolution path is forked instead of extending the MT-034 CrossRef machinery, duplicating the 'melt-together' substrate and fragmenting the interconnection invariant.

RISK-003: The two failure modes are conflated — a live-endpoint 404 (record not found) is mis-reported as the `LocusReadApiUnavailable` typed blocker (or vice versa), producing false blockers or hiding a genuine missing-API gap.
RISK-004: Resolution or reverse lookup runs on the egui render path, blocking the frame thread and freezing the UI on documents with many `locus://` refs.
RISK-005: The `locus_ref` node is not declared as an allowed node type, so saveRichDocument strips it silently and the cross-reference is lost on reload.

RISK-006: Scope creep into the backend — adding/scaffolding a Locus read endpoint, mutating/transitioning a WP/MT record, or introducing a local SQLite cache — violating read-only, no-backend-rewrite, and PostgreSQL/EventLedger-only constraints.
RISK-007: Navigation creates a new ad-hoc channel for `open-locus-ref` instead of reusing the MT-030 navigation bus / WP-011 command bus, fragmenting the shell navigation invariant.

RISK-008: AccessKit author_id collisions when the same WP/MT id appears in multiple chips, or when reverse-lookup rows for the same document are not de-duplicated, making swarm-addressable ids ambiguous.
RISK-009: Clicking an unresolved/blocked chip panics or no-ops silently because the resolution result is None/blocked and the `open-locus-ref` path assumes a resolved record.
RISK-010: WP/MT ids embedded in URLs are not correctly encoded (ids contain hyphens and uppercase segments such as WP-KERNEL-012), producing 404s that masquerade as 'record not found'.
minimum_controls: MC-001: Grep gate fails the MT if a second `locus://` parser/normalizer is defined locally; a test asserts the MT-032 normalizer is imported and used as the single key for both resolution and reverse lookup.
MC-002: Grep/diff gate confirms the `locus_ref` node extends the MT-034 CrossRef node/chip/resolution path (shared helpers imported); no parallel cross-ref node or resolution service is forked.

MC-003: Two distinct tests cover the failure modes: (a) live-endpoint 404 -> greyed 'unresolved' chip, no blocker; (b) endpoint absent from the reachable surface -> `LocusReadApiUnavailable` typed blocker naming the endpoint. The two paths must not be conflated.

MC-004: A test asserts `show()`/render never performs network I/O; resolution and reverse lookup run off-render-path (async or via the existing client request mechanism) and render only cached struct state.

MC-005: `locus_ref` is declared as an explicit allowed node type with a save+reload round-trip test asserting attrs { kind, id, normalized } are preserved; if the backend rejects the node type, a typed blocker is raised rather than silent stripping.

MC-006: Diff/dependency gate confirms no `src/backend/**` changes, no new endpoints, no Locus mutation, and no SQLite dependency added; backend access is read-only via the two bound GET endpoints plus the reused MT-034 reverse-lookup mechanism.
MC-007: Grep gate confirms `open-locus-ref` flows through the reused command bus + MT-030 navigation bus seam (FnMut(NavTarget) / dispatch); no new navigation channel is created.

MC-008: AccessKit ids are de-duplicated and derived from (kind,id) for chips and (document_id) for reverse-lookup rows; a test asserts no duplicate author_id in the tree dump and registration via the WP-011 id registry.
MC-009: A test asserts clicking an unresolved/blocked chip fires the dispatch (or no-ops gracefully) without panic when the resolution result is None/blocked.

MC-010: Apply percent-encoding to all WP/MT ids before embedding them in URLs; a unit test exercises an id containing hyphens and uppercase segments (e.g. WP-KERNEL-012) and asserts the resolution call targets the correct, correctly-encoded path.
